chapter_title.ar: باب الرُّكُوعِ وَ مَا يُقَالُ فِيهِ مِنَ التَّسْبِيحِ وَ الدُّعَاءِ فِيهِ وَ إِذَا رَفَعَ الرَّأْسَ مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – The Rukū and what is said during it from the Glorification, and the supplication during it, and when the head is raised from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/124)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/124)
text: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَرْكَعَ وَ تَسْجُدَ فَارْفَعْ يَدَيْكَ وَ كَبِّرْ ثُمَّ ارْكَعْ وَ اسْجُدْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Zurara who said,
‘Abu Ja’farasws said: ‘When you intend to go into Rukū and perform Sajdah, so raise your hands and exclaim Takbīr, then go into Rukū and perform Sajdah’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When you decide to assume Ruku‘ (bowing down on one’s knees) and Sajdah (prostration) position, raise your hands, say Takbir (Allah is great beyond description), then assume Ruku‘ (bowing down on one’s knees) and Sajdah (prostration) position.’”
